enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias toggle preserves semantics
enforced.oracle: After toggling RNEXT between '=' and the explicit RNAME value, the parsed alignment record should be semantically equivalent: the reference name of the mate is preserved, and all downstream fields (PNEXT, TLEN, flags) remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: RNEXT (if not `*' or `=') must be present in one of the SQ-SN tag. This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 exclusivity violation on mapped read must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when FLAG 0x4 is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length mismatch with SEQ must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when sum of query-consuming CIGAR ops (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ); silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a '*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.
If not a '*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation in paired reads must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when two reads of a paired template carry same-signed TLEN values; the spec mandates opposite signs for the two ends; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.mr_name: Query method coordinate invariance under header shuffle
enforced.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside @SQ records, the parsed alignment records' coordinate-related query methods (getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd, getStart, getEnd, getReferenceName) must return identical values. The coordinate system is defined by POS and @SQ SN/LN, which are unchanged by intra-line field reordering.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1.
SN* & Reference sequence name. The SN tags and all individual AN names in all @SQ lines must be distinct. The value of this field is used in the alignment records in RNAME and RNEXT fields.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P canonicalizes RNEXT to the explicit RNAME form; the spec allows both '=' and explicit RNAME as equivalent representations, so a write-roundtrip must preserve the semantic content regardless of which form is used

evidence.quote: RNEXT: ... This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias invariance under canonicalize_rnext_equals_alias
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getMateReferenceName, getReadPairedFlag, getReadName, getFlags. The RNEXT '=' alias is a spec-allowed synonym for 'same as RNAME' (SAMv1 §1.4.7), so toggling between explicit RNAME and '=' must not change any query method's scalar output.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.
This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Unmapped flag query invariance under normalize_unmapped_record_fields
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadUnmappedFlag, getMappingQuality, getFlags, getReadName. Normalizing MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads (per SAMv1 spec) is semantics-preserving since MAPQ is undefined when 0x4 is set; query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag query invariance under strip_mate_flags_if_unpaired
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadPairedFlag, getFirstOfPairFlag, getSecondOfPairFlag, getProperPairFlag, getFlags. When FLAG bit 0x1 is unset, mate-context bits 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80 are meaningless per SAMv1 §1.4; clearing them is semantics-preserving so query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.
If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge query invariance under split_or_merge_adjacent_cigar_ops
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getCigarLength, getCigarString, getLengthOnReference, getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd. Splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths, so query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.
Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: Header comment shuffle query invariance under shuffle_co_comments
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadName, getFlags, getReferenceName, getAlignmentStart. @CO comment lines have no ordering semantics per SAMv1 §1.3; shuffling them is semantics-preserving so all record-level query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: @CO & One-line text comment. Unordered multiple @CO lines are allowed.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation query invariance under permute_optional_tag_fields
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadName, getFlags, getReferenceName, getAlignmentStart, getMappingQuality. Shuffling optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields (columns 12+) does not change the 11 mandatory fields, so all mandatory-field query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each alignment record; the set of TAG:TYPE:VALUE tuples is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @HD header subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); the set of TAG:VALUE pairs on the @HD line is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ header subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); the set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @SQ line is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @RG header subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); the set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @RG line is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG header subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); the set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @PG line is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @CO comment line order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly exempted from the TAG:VALUE format; the spec treats them as free-text comments but does not explicitly state they have no ordering semantics — this is implied by their exemption from the structured format

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ header record order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); the set of @SQ lines is preserved but their order is permuted

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: @SQ line order defines the coordinate sort order (SAMv1 @HD SO:coordinate), so reordering @SQ lines changes coordinate sort semantics for coordinate-sorted files; this MR is only valid for unsorted/queryname-sorted files or when the oracle accounts for the reindexing

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: Uppercasing the SEQ field must not change the parsed alignment's semantics. The spec regex permits both upper and lower case letters in SEQ.

evidence.quote: The case-insensitive base codes `=ACMGRSVTWYHKDBN' are mapped to [0,15] respectively with all other characters mapping to `N' (value 15).

ambiguity_flags: The spec says base codes are case-insensitive in BAM encoding, but the SAM text format regex for SEQ is not explicitly stated as case-insensitive. However, the BAM encoding rule implies case-insensitivity at the semantic level.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: Removing zero-length CIGAR operations and merging adjacent identical operations must not change the alignment's semantics. The total reference-consumed and query-consumed lengths remain identical.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: adjacent CIGAR operations should be different
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops, but the Recommended Practice says adjacent operations should be different, implying zero-length ops are semantically meaningless.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR split-or-merge preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: Splitting a CIGAR operation into two identical operations or merging adjacent identical operations must preserve the total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths, and thus the alignment semantics.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address splitting/merging CIGAR ops, but the total consumed length is the semantically meaningful quantity.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR M to =/X canonicalization via MD tag normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: Rewriting M CIGAR ops into = (match) and X (mismatch) ops using the MD tag preserves all alignment semantics: POS, end coordinate, SEQ, QUAL, and the NM tag (recomputed per SAM spec) remain consistent. The total reference- and query-consuming lengths are unchanged.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The MD tag is an optional field; not all alignments carry it. The transform is a no-op when MD is absent.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag with illegal type character must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when an optional tag's type character is not one of the valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B); silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The SAMtags section (sec:alnaux) defines the valid type characters as A, i, f, Z, H, B; the exact list of valid type characters is referenced indirectly via 'see Section~sec:alnaux' rather than enumerated inline in the main spec body

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound check invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing the corresponding @SQ LN by +shift, the alignment end (POS + sum of reference-consuming CIGAR lengths - 1) must still be <= LN for every mapped record. The set of valid alignments (those not exceeding LN) must remain identical before and after the shift.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.
LN* & Reference sequence length. Range: $[1,\,2^31-1]$

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (advisory, not mandatory) for POS+CIGAR exceeding LN; some parsers may reject while others accept such records

quarantine.mr_name: BAM round-trip coordinate invariance
quarantine.oracle: After round-tripping through BAM, the POS field of every alignment record must remain unchanged. The BAM binary format uses 0-based coordinates internally (POS-1), so the round-trip must correctly convert between 1-based SAM and 0-based BAM coordinates without off-by-one errors.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.

BIN is calculated using the reg2bin() function in Section <ref>. For mapped reads this uses -1 (i.e., 0-based left position) and the alignment end point using the alignment length from the CIGAR string.

0-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [2,7). The BAM, BCFv2, BED, and PSL formats are using the 0-based coordinate system.

quarantine.mr_name: CRAM round-trip coordinate invariance
quarantine.oracle: After round-tripping through CRAM, the POS field of every alignment record must remain unchanged. CRAM internally stores positions in a 0-based coordinate system, so the round-trip must correctly convert between 1-based SAM and 0-based CRAM coordinates without off-by-one errors.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.

0-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [2,7). The BAM, BCFv2, BED, and PSL formats are using the 0-based coordinate system.

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may collapse =/X CIGAR ops to M, which changes CIGAR but not POS; the canonical normalizer's cram_safe mode accounts for this

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record POS=0 invariance under header reordering
quarantine.oracle: For records with FLAG bit 0x4 set (unmapped) and no coordinate, POS must remain 0 after reordering @SQ header lines. The POS=0 sentinel for unmapped reads without coordinate must be invariant under header reorganization.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate. If POS is 0, no assumptions can be made about RNAME and CIGAR.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be set as * and POS as 0' (advisory) for unmapped templates; some parsers may accept non-zero POS for unmapped reads

quarantine.mr_name: Header subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts @HD subtags; the spec states the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within header lines is not significant, so shuffling them and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SQ record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts subtags within each @SQ line; the spec states the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within header lines is not significant, so shuffling @SQ subtags and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: PG record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts subtags within each @PG line; the spec states the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within header lines is not significant, so shuffling @PG subtags and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: RG record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts subtags within each @RG line; the spec states the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within header lines is not significant, so shuffling @RG subtags and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: CO comment line shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts @CO lines; @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics, so shuffling them and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format ` TAG:VALUE' where TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of VALUE.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sorts optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields; the SAM spec does not mandate ordering of optional fields, so permuting them and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state optional field ordering is insignificant, but no ordering semantics are defined

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P normalizes CIGAR by removing zero-length ops; adjacent CIGAR operations should be different per recommended practice, and zero-length ops carry no alignment information

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: This is a recommended practice (ADVISORY), not a mandatory spec rule

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P uppercases SEQ; the SAM spec permits both upper and lower case in SEQ, so normalizing case and round-tripping must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — SEQ and QUAL encoding] The case-insensitive base codes `=ACMGRSVTWYHKDBN' are mapped to [0,15] respectively with all other characters mapping to `N' (value 15).

quarantine.mr_name: Split or merge adjacent CIGAR ops round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P normalizes CIGAR by merging adjacent identical ops; splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations preserves total query-consumed length and alignment semantics

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: This is a recommended practice (ADVISORY), not a mandatory spec rule

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization query invariance under normalize_seq_case
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadString, getBaseQualityString, getReadLength, getReadName. SAMv1 §1.4.10 states 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' for SEQ, so uppercasing is semantics-preserving and all query methods must return identical scalar values.

evidence.quote: An `=' denotes the base is identical to the reference base. No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' which is permissive but does not explicitly mandate case-insensitivity; however, the canonical normalizer uppercases SEQ, making this a valid semantics-preserving transform for conformant parsers.

quarantine.mr_name: Header subtag shuffle query invariance under shuffle_hd_subtags
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadName, getFlags, getReferenceName, getAlignmentStart. SAMv1 §1.3 does not order TAG:VALUE pairs inside @HD; shuffling them is semantics-preserving so all record-level query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that @HD subtags are unordered, but the canonical normalizer sorts them, implying order is not semantically meaningful.
